The winners think where there's one, there's a formula, there's a model, and there could be a hundred, there could be a thousand, there could be an unlimited number if they just followed the model. So where you grew up, the conditions you grew up under, whether you had supportive parents or unsupportive parents, none of that impacts on what you decide about who you're going to be today. No pre-determination. We've already established today experience doesn't matter, expertise doesn't matter. So none of that determines what you want to do today. Now we say look there are some real limits, but they are far away from what most people operate under. I mean, as a practical matter I can't go be the quarterback of the Dallas Cowboys. Cleveland Browns are the Oakland Raiders maybe, but you know the Dallas, I'd probably get a tryout at least at the Raiders, but quarterback in the Cowboys is probably out. It's a little old to really start thinking about that, but I could buy them. I mean I could own them, but you know far within the real limits about what do you want to do. For most people what they want to do is well within their grasp. Sometimes there's no thing, what do you want to be known for? Well you decide that. Nothing prior to today matters, doesn't have to control it, nobody else gets to decide what that is. You can decide right now, tonight you can sit down with a pad and make a list of the five things you want to be known for and go to work to become known for them and make that happen. It's a very mechanical process. We've talked about a lot of it today. How you want to be perceived, how you want to conduct business and live, who you want to associate with, who you want to let into your world, who do you not want to let into your world. One of my first speaking gigs, and it's still one of my favorite stories about this, I was doing real basic personal growth, motivational one on one stuff. I was speaking for free any place they'd let me speak for free in feedings, and so a lot of the speaking was in office stuff. So I'm speaking in this real estate office at their morning sales training meeting, and this is the time when meeting attendance was compulsory so the brokerages could actually insist they'd be there. I can't do that today. And there was like eight people at eight desks, so they're all sitting at their desks, yeah. And I read in a newspaper and drinking coffee and all that stuff, and I'm speaking it as horrible. It's all over with, this woman comes up to me and she says, I agree with everything you said but, which is, you know, it's the but that gets you, right? So I can't tell you, I'd like to have a penny for every time I've heard, I agree with everything you said but, or you were right, but I'm still going to keep doing it the wrong way in spite of the fact that you were right or not. So she comes up, she says, I agree with everything you said, but I have this problem. What's your problem? I want to be a positive thinker, I'm telling you, I was doing very basic. But the person at the desk next to me, here's what they do every morning. They come to work, they get the newspaper, they get coffee, and they read aloud and talk about all the bad news that's in the newspaper. So that person every day gives me all the bad economic news and fundamentally all of the reasons why I'm not going to be able to make any money today, this week, this month, whatever. And this goes on for like a half an hour every morning. I said, change desks, move your desk. You know, you're not ankle change to the guy if he's a toxic person, move. If they won't let you change desks, change offices. There's 10,000 real estate brokerages in Phoenix, they'll all welcome you and hang your license on the wall. So you decide who you let in. You decide who you sit next to except on Southwest. You decide who you hang out with. You don't, you know, we don't get to pick our relatives, but you can even make some decisions there about at least how much time you spend. You decide where you want to work and live. I used to get that all the time when I lived in Phoenix. I was traveling, somebody on a plane, oh, you live in Phoenix. Oh, I wish I could live in Phoenix. I live in the Ruta Bag in Minnesota where it's always 42 degrees below zero and there's 863 pounds of snow and oh, this is awful. I said, well, they got them green signs overhead with arrows on them. They tell you how to get out of town. You don't even need, you know, a wizard gizmo. There's signs. They show you, leave. You know? I mean, and you decide when you want to do things. So the first thing that makes money move to people is decision. Somebody in here mentioned LMI earlier to me on a break. I mean, Paul Meyers' great quote was, you know, if you're not getting what you want out of life, it's probably because your goals aren't clearly enough to find. Well, I mean, he's basically right. It certainly is not for any shortage of opportunity. We just saw a bunch of complete fruit loops. Getting paid $3,000 to go in and move the furniture around. And it wouldn't surprise me if she was booked out. It ain't for lack of opportunity. See, one of the messages of what you just saw, the bottled water, the Feng Shui, all of it, is there's a ton of money out there moving around all the time. People, their money's very fluid. Money's moving. There's all kinds of money out there. Go look, walk around, go to a mall, go drive around the shopping center on Friday night, try and get into a restaurant. Go to a, when the hell the last time, Carla, were we in that Mac store? I mean, I forget when we were there together, but it was like an off day. It wasn't like Christmas or anything. I mean, and it was like dinner hour or something. You walk in this Mac store, and I mean, people are in line 20 deep at the registers with piles of this stuff. Laptops and gizmos and I mean, you would think they were buying popcorn at Kmart. They're piling up this stuff. Big screen TVs, can't keep them in stock. Best buy store in my neighborhood, right before Super Bowl, actually put a big sign up on the building, we're out of televisions.
